definition: A title for the Buddha that is usually best left untranslated in source-facing work.
notes: The project leaves Sugata untranslated by default because it functions as a title and English renderings tend to sound forced or overinterpretive. A short gloss is acceptable when the line needs help on first occurrence.

context_rules.context: In most source-facing and doctrinal contexts
context_rules.notes: Default project practice is to leave the title untranslated.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where a gloss is necessary
context_rules.notes: Use sparingly and only as a gloss.

example_phrases.notes: Common titular usage in praise formulas.
